steps.title: Use the 3-4-5 triangle shortcut
steps.content:
From angle $L$, tangent is opposite over adjacent:
$$\tan(L)=\frac{JK}{LK}=\frac{3}{4}$$
So the legs are in a $3:4$ ratio. That is the leg ratio of a $3$-$4$-$5$ right triangle.

steps.title: Scale from the hypotenuse
steps.content:
The hypotenuse of the basic $3$-$4$-$5$ triangle is $5$. In the figure, the hypotenuse $LJ$ is $90$, so the scale factor is:
$$90\div5=18$$

steps.title: Find JK
steps.content:
$JK$ is the side opposite angle $L$, so it corresponds to the $3$ side. Therefore:
$$JK=3(18)=54$$
That matches choice C.
